_comment: Ground truth annotation — PRISMA checklist for Moraes et al. 2022

article.title: Hydroxychloroquine for treatment of COVID-19 patients: a systematic review and meta-analysis of randomized controlled trials
article.authors: Moraes VY, Marra AR, Matos LL, Serpa Neto A, Rizzo LV, Cendoroglo Neto M, Lenza M

study_type.notes: Systematic review and meta-analysis of RCTs. Follows PRISMA guidelines and Cochrane handbook. Registered in PROSPERO.

1.notes: Title: 'systematic review and meta-analysis of randomized controlled trials'

2.notes: Structured abstract with Objective, Methods, Results, Conclusion

3.notes: Rationale: HCQ attention, lack of good evidence, need for systematic evaluation of RCTs

4.notes: Objective: evaluate efficacy and safety of HCQ for COVID-19 patients (mortality, serious adverse events)

5.notes: PROSPERO registration CRD42020197070 mentioned; conducted per PRISMA and Cochrane handbook

6.notes: Inclusion: RCTs, adults ≥18, COVID-19, HCQ vs placebo/standard of care. Exclusion: prevention/prophylaxis studies

7.notes: CENTRAL, MEDLINE, EMBASE, LILACS searched; also WHO Clinical Trials Registry and reference lists

8.notes: Full MEDLINE search strategy with MeSH terms and text words provided

9.notes: Two authors independently selected studies, disagreements resolved by discussion/consensus, third author if needed

10.notes: Two authors independently extracted data using specific extraction form; study design, participants, interventions, outcomes, risk of bias

11.notes: Cochrane Collaboration Risk of Bias tool: random sequence generation, allocation concealment, blinding, incomplete data, selective reporting, other bias

12.notes: Primary: all-cause mortality. Secondary: serious adverse events

13a.notes: Random-effects model, Mantel-Haenszel method, risk ratio with 95% CI

13b.notes: Cochran's Q statistic and I² for heterogeneity (I²>50% threshold)

14.notes: GRADE approach for quality of evidence: high/moderate/low/very low

15.notes: Funnel plots for publication bias assessment

16.notes: 3,014 articles identified → 44 screened → 17 assessed → 9 included, 8 excluded. Figure 1 flowchart

17.notes: Table 1: country, site, drug, HCQ regimens, control, age, sample sizes, diagnosis method per study

18.notes: Figure 2: risk of bias assessment (summarized and individual per study)

19.notes: Forest plots (Figures 3-5) for mortality and adverse events with pooled RR and 95% CI

20.notes: Forest plot (Figure 4) with risk difference showing individual study estimates

21.notes: Table 2: Summary of findings with GRADE assessment (low for mortality, very low for adverse events)

22a.notes: Summary: no significant mortality reduction with HCQ (RR 1.09, 95% CI 0.99-1.19), no increase in adverse events

22b.notes: Limitations: heterogeneous populations, different HCQ dose criteria, RECOVERY trial dominates weight, only 2 double-blinded

22c.notes: No explicit discussion of implications for future research beyond general statement about properly designed RCTs

23.notes: Conclusion: HCQ not efficacious in reducing mortality of COVID-19 patients

25.notes: No specific statement about funding for the review itself

26.notes: No conflicts of interest statement for the review authors
